title: Dinosaurs & Paleontology
objective: Students will explore dinosaurs & paleontology concepts including Fossils as Evidence.

steps.content: Today we're going to learn about Fossils as Evidence. Get ready for an adventure!
Analyse and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of organisms and environments that existed long ago
Let me show you something cool. Explain that fossils are preserved remains or traces of organisms that lived long ago
If you sees a fossil of a fish in a rock on a hillside, can they explain that the area must have been underwater millions of years ago?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Fossils as Evidence: Explain that fossils are preserved remains or traces of organisms that lived long ago Use fossil evidence to make inferences about past organisms and their environments
Let's discover the amazing world of Weather vs climate!
Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world, distinguishing between weather and climate
Time to try this out: Define weather as day-to-day conditions and climate as the long-term pattern of weather in an area
Can you explain the difference between weather and climate, and describe why the Arctic is always cold while tropical areas are always warm?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Weather vs climate: Define weather as day-to-day conditions and climate as the long-term pattern of weather in an area Describe at least three different climate types (tropical, temperate, polar, desert) with examples
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Seasonal changes (age 8+)!
Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season
Let me show you something cool. Collect and organise weather data in a table (temperature, precipitation, cloud cover)
Can you collect weather data over several weeks, put it in a chart, and use it to describe what weather is typical for that time of year?

Great job! Today we learned about Seasonal changes (age 8+). Collect and organise weather data in a table (temperature, precipitation, cloud cover) Create a graphical display (bar chart, pictogram) showing weather patterns for a season
